last_item: Pooler — the enable-confirmation copy ('Your apps then connect to …') reads as if the pooler reroutes apps automatically; users may not realize they must change their app's connection string (med/S).
last_result: SHIPPED. Reworded the 'Enable the connection pooler?' confirm dialog's closing paragraph from the passive 'Your apps then connect to <addr> instead of Postgres directly' (reads as auto-rerouting) to lead with 'Enabling won't move any app over by itself' + the explicit manual step ('change an app's connection string to point at <addr> in place of the direct Postgres host. Apps you don't repoint keep connecting to Postgres directly, unchanged'), preserving the 'no restart / no data touched' honesty line. Pure copy reword in an existing dialog — no new UI/controls. Added two enable-test assertions for the new copy. Panel: 3 SHIP outright (UX heuristics — 'classic Error-Prevention failure fixed at the moment it matters'; Priya — 'corrects the one mechanical fact I'd otherwise get wrong'; restraint critic — 'word swap that fixes a genuine why-did-nothing-happen trap, earns itself'). Sam REJECTED — not on my paragraph but on an ADJACENT contradiction: the bullet 'Route N roles through it' implied auto-routing, clashing with the new 'you must repoint' paragraph. Cheap, clearly right, and it strengthened this same fix, so per the contract I addressed it in-iteration: reworded the bullet to 'Allow N roles to connect through the pooler' (also more accurate — enabling just adds the role to the pooler userlist) + updated its test assertion; re-ran Sam → SHIP. Final panel 4 SHIP, no blockers. Gates: typecheck OK, 138 tests OK, build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox — snap-confine blocks in-sandbox). Lessons recorded: (1) confirm copy for an action whose effect needs manual follow-up must name the follow-up in the imperative — don't describe the end-state as if it arrives automatically; (2) when correcting a mental-model bug in copy, scan the surrounding copy in the same view for other phrasings that assert the old wrong model — they'll now clash; reconcile together. Next top item: Dashboard — 'Connections' shown twice (Postgres card + Server card); drop the duplicate (med/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
